def: s.m. Fourth part, quarter; the fourth part of a ser, a weight of about half a pound; — pāʼo ānā, s.m. One fourth of an anna, one pice
more: s. m. A quarter, a fourth part. ¶ pāw, see پانو pāṅw.
subs: adj. A ser; about a ser
pāʼo-ṭakī, s.f. The fee of a qānungo allowed by Government at the rate of four annas per hundred rupees on the net revenue of each district, to defray the expenses of the establishment
s.m. The fourth part of a circle, a quadrant
s.f. A loaf of bread made in the European way, and weighing a pāʼo or quarter of a ser, i.e. about half a pound.

def: s.m. = pāhū, q.v. s.m. Person, individual; a contemptuous form of address.
(dakh.) To step by rule, to walk stately.
(dakh.) i. q. قاعدي سي قدم ڐالنا ḳāʼiﻌde se ḳadam ḍālnā, To step by rule.

adj. S. पाद A quarter; a fourth part. pāo ānā , One-fourth of an anna; one pice. pāo chakkar ; A. ruba dāërah , n. m. ( Geom .). A quadrant; the fourth part of a circle. pāo ser, pāo bhar , adj. A quarter of a seer. pāolā, pāvlā , n. m. A quarter of a rupee; four annas. pāolī ; Rus. pawe'lī , n. f. A four anna piece ( chavannī ).
s.m. (f. -ī), A quarter of any coin; a quarter of a rupee, a four anna piece.
more: s. m. A quarter of any coin (as, of a rupee, &c.).

def: s.m. = pāṅw and pāṅʼoṅ, q.v.; — also (for pāṅwoṅ), 'feet.' s.m. Foot; leg; foot-print; basis, foundation, root; footing, foothold; part, lot, portion; interference, meddling; steadiness, decision (istiqlāl); responsibility, liability
subs: v.n. The foot to be dislocated
pāṅw uṭhākar ćalnā, v.n. To go or walk quickly, to step out
(-kā), To move (one) from his place, intention, or resolution
v.n. To lose one's footing, be carried off one's legs; to run away, take to flight; to be routed
v.n. The foot to slip; to lose one's balance; to go wrong or astray
(-meṅ), To take the lead, go ahead; to overstep, exceed bounds; to desist from one's former courses
v.n. To be with child, be pregnant
v.n. To suffer numbness or heaviness of the feet
v.n. To have a finger (in); to have a share (in); to be responsible (for)
adv. Afoot, on foot, walking; — pāṅw-pāṅw phirnā, pāṅw-pāṅw ćalnā, pāṅw-pāṅw ḍolnā, v.n. To walk, to toddle (a child)
v.n. To walk in the steps (of another); to imitate or adopt the conduct (of another); to place one leg over the other, to sit cross-legged, to sit at ease
pāṅw-par tesha mār-lenā = apne pānw-meṅ āp kulhārī mārnā
v.n. Of oneself to spoil one's own affair; to injure or ruin oneself, to cut one's own throat
(-ke), To fall at the feet (of; — see pāṅw paṛnā and pāṅw pakaṛnā)
(-ke), To fall at the feet (of), prostrate oneself; to entreat submissively, beseech, implore
v.n. To stretch the legs full length, lie comfortably or at ease, take it easy; to stretch out the legs in death, to die
(-ke), To lay hold of or seize the feet (of); to fall at the feet (of); — to beseech submissively; to prevent (one) from going
(-ke), To worship, or to kiss, the feet (of), to do honour or reverence (to); — to shun, avoid
v.n. The feet to swell; to be tired or weary; to be perplexed, or embarrassed
v.n. (or phūṅk-phūṅk-ke pāṅw rakhnā), To move cautiously or warily (in), to do anything carefully; to tread softly (as on cinders)
v.n. (A woman) to visit her parent's, or another, house, after childbirth
v.n. To sleep with the legs stretched out full length; to sleep at ease, or in peace and security; to be free from care, be perfectly contented and happy
v.n. To be full length, or at ease, &c. = pāṅw pasārnā, q.v.; — to raise one's demands; to be importunate; to be insatiable; to insist, be obstinate; to be exacting
v.n. To stamp with impatience, or anger; to struggle vainly against difficulties; to throw about the legs (in pain, anguish, or the agonies of death)
s.f. lit. 'An ant under the foot'; a poor, helpless, or miserable creature
pāṅw-tale-kī nikalnā, v.n. To feel the ground slipping, as it were, from under one's feet; to be stunned or staggered
v.n. To trample under foot, to crush; to give pain (to), to annoy
v.n. To wear out one's legs in vain; to be marched or trotted about in vain; to be tired; to desist from visiting (a person); to run vainly after (a person); to visit (one) very often
v.n. To persevere firmly (in a course, an enterprise, or a resolution)
v.n. To plant the feet firmly, to stand firmly; to get a footing or foot-hold
v.n. To have a good footing, to stand firmly
s.f. Rubbing or pressing the feet
v.n. The foot to slip, or give way; to totter, to become unstable
(-par or meṅ), To set foot (on); to come (into), step (in), enter; to land: to go or step forward, advance; to prepare (for), enter (upon), to begin

(-ke), lit. 'To drink the water in which one's feet have been washed'; to do honour or reverence (to); to place perfect confidence (in); to show respect or affection (for), to regard with the greatest respect, to look up (to)
v.n. The foot to slip or totter; to slip, to lose (one's) footing; to give way (a foundation, an army, &c.)
v.n. To rub the feet together; to go about foolishly and unprofitably; to be in the agonies of death
v.n. To be unable to stand (through excessive joy), to be unsteady on the feet (through joy)
(or, more com., zamīn-par pāṅw na rakhnā), v.n. To tread the air; to walk on stilts; to walk like a swell; to show off
v.n. The foot to be numbed, the foot to sleep
v.n. To stick close (to one), to watch (one) closely
v.n. To be near or close (to)
v.n. To occupy a fixed habitation; to adopt a new resolution
v.n. To have a tremor of the legs; to totter; to fear to attempt anything
(-ke), To convict one by his own arguments
(or, more com., gor-meṅ pāṅw laṭkānā), v.n. To have one foot in the grave; to be advanced in years
v.n. The skin of the feet to be rubbed away or worn; to be always on the tramp; to undergo useless labour
pāṅw laraznā, v.n. = pāṅw kāṅpnā, q.v.
v.n. To be always on the move
v.n. To apply henna to the feet; to be unable to set foot on the ground
v.n. To show the cloven hoof; to exceed one's proper limits; to betray one's evil disposition; to begin one's pranks; to be a ringleader (in a criminal action); to withdraw (from, -se), back out (of)
v.n. To be ready on one foot; to be ready to start (on an enterprise, &c.); to be ready and willing
v.n. To stand in the breach; to risk one's life (for another)
adv. Softly, quietly, gently; slily, stealthily, with a cat-like tread.

def: Same as پانو pāṅw, q. v. or the plur. inflection (for پانوون). پاؤن پرتيشه مارلينا pāʼoṅ par tesha mār lenā, Of oneʼs self to spoil oneʼs own affair. پاؤن دهول pāʼoṅ dhūl, Misfortune: see آسيب āseb.
s.m. Name of a plant that grows in stagnant water.

v.t. = pānā, q.v. v.t. To get, acquire, receive, obtain, attain, accept, reach; to pick up, earn, gain, reap; to procure, secure; to overtake, to come upon, meet with, encounter; to enjoy, experience, undergo, suffer, feel; — s.m. Getting, finding, &c.; — dues, outstandings
more: v. a. See پانا pānā, sign. 1.
subs: in Acquisitives — i.e. when annexed to an inflected infinitive or a verbal base — is neuter, and signifies 'to be allowed'; 'to get permission'; 'to get' to do a thing; 'to be able' to do; e.g. woh dekhne pāyā, 'he was allowed to see,' or 'he got to see,' &c.; we ān-pāye, 'they were permitted to come,' &c.; bāt aise dhīraj-se kar ki wah na sun-pāwe, 'Speak so softly that he be not able to hear'; — hone pānā, 'To let be or happen'; kisī jīv-ko pīṛā na hone-pāwe, 'Let pain be occasioned to no living thing').

def: s.f. = pāṅwṛī, q.v. s.f. Pattens (syn. khaṛāʼoṅ); sandals.
n. f. English loaves.
(see پانا) s. f. Income: see آمدني āmdanī.
s.f. That which is gained or got; gain, income (syn. āmdanī).
s. (dakh.) Bezoar stone ( Harris ): see پاد زهر pād-zahr.
s.m. Evil, sin, vice, crime, wickedness, transgression, guilt; immorality, adultery, fornication
more: n. m. 1. Sin; guilt; misdeed; vice. 1. Jāp ke birte pāp . Prov. Sin leans on devotion. (More sins are committed in the faith that more prayers and sacrifice will wipe them out). 2. Jiskā pāp, uskā bāp . Prov. A man's sin is his father (master). 3. Pāpī ke man meṅ pāp hī base . Prov. Sin only dwelleth in the sinner's mind. 4. Tulsī, pichhle pāp se Har charchā na suhāë, Jaise jur ke tāp se bhojan kī ruchi jāë . Tulsī Dās. Your past birth's sins, O Tulsī, make Har's worship an offence, As is distaste for food of fever's heat a consequence. 5. Sāṅch barābar tap nahīṅ, aur jhūṭ barābar pāp, Jāke man meṅ sāṅch hai, tā ke man meṅ Āp . Dohā. Like truth no sacrifice, like to a lie no sin, The mind where truth doth dwell's the mind that God lives in. 2. Adultery; fornication; immorality; misconduct. Pāp kī bāt maiṅ jānat nāhīṅ; Maiṅ ne karo hai phakīrī bhes kuā pe, Ṭhārī sukhāūṅ kes kūā pe . Wom. …
subs: adj. & s.m.f. Of a wicked or vicious disposition, sinful, criminal, reprobate; — sinner, reprobate, wretch
adj. & s.m. Following an evil course of life, wicked, vicious; — wicked or vicious person
s.m. Pursuit of sin or vice; — pāp-āćaraṇ karnā, v.n. To lead a life of sin
v.n. To reap the reward of sins committed in a former birth
adj. Evil-minded, of corrupt intellect, sinful, ungodly, wicked, depraved, graceless
pāp kamānā, pāp mol-lenā, v.n. To commit sin, do wrong; to hug a sin; to bring calamity (upon)
adj. Bearing evil fruit, having evil consequences; inauspicious
pāp lagnā (-ko), To be stained with sin, to sin, to commit sin
s.f. The creeping plant Clypea hernandifolia
adj. & s.m. Looking at faults, censorious; — a malevolent person
adj. & s.m. Conscious of sin, knowing an act to be wicked; — one who is conscious of sin; one who is conversant with sin
s.m. The misery, or the punishment, consequent on sinning
pāp kāṭnā (-kā), To put away sin, to wipe out sin, to pardon, grant absolution or deliverance from sin
pāp kaṭnā, v.n. Sin to be blotted out, to be pardoned or absolved
s.m. Any bad disease (as leprosy, small-pox, syphilis, &c.) considered as the penalty of sin in a former life
pāp-kārī, pāp-kar, pāp-kartā, pāp kartr̤i, s.m. Sinner, criminal, guilty person
s.m. = pāp-kar, q.v.; (also) evil action, misdeed, crime
v.n. To sin; to commit adultery or fornication

vulg. pāp-girah, s.m. Inauspicious planet; any ill-omened aspect of the heavenly bodies (such as the conjunction of the sun or Budh with the moon in her last quarter); Rahu, Saturn, Mars; — calamity, misfortune, fatal destiny
adj. Destroying, effacing, or expiating sin
(-ko), To impute fault (to), to blame, charge, accuse; to calumniate
v.n. To confess sin or fault
adj. = pap-buddhi, q.v.; — pāp-matī, s.f. Sinful woman
adj. & s.m. Liberated or absolved from guilt; one who has been freed from sin or crime
vulg. pāp-moćan or pāp-moćhan, s.m. Liberation from sin, absolution, pardon, salvation — pāp mol-lenā, v.n. = pāp bisānā, q.v.
wety: Borrowed from Sanskrit पाप (pāpa).

def: s.m. Weevil, an insect bred in rice ( = piṭārī).
more: n. m. A weevil; an insect bred in rice.
s. m. A weevil, an insect bred in rice. See H. پٿاري piṭārī.

def: s.m. Father ( = bābā); the Pope.
s.m. The Papaw tree, and its fruit, Carica papaya; a child's whistle ( = papīhā).
more: n. m. 1. A child's whistle or pipe. 2. A germinated mango stone ( amol ). 3. A fruit ( Carica papayā . Wat).
s. m. 1. A sort of childʼs whistle. 2. Name of a tree and of its fruit (the Papaw tree, Carica papaya).

def: s.m. = paparmās, q.v. s.m. The name given in Southern India to the Pompelmose or Pumelo (in Northern India it is called ćakotrā).
s.m. Crust, thin layer; scale, scab; thin crisp cake made of any pulse (esp. of mūṅg); thin light soil, fertile soil
more: n. m. S. पर्पट. 1. A crust; a thin layer; a scab. 2. A thin crisp cake made of any pulse. 1. Mār līyā hai pāpaṛ-wāle ko . Prov. I have overcome the pieman. (I have mastered the difficulty). 2. Pān sā patlā, chāṅd sā chaklā . Riddle. Ans. Pāpaṛ . As thin as betel leaf, as round as the full moon. pāpaṛ bel'nā, pāpaṛ pīṭnā , v. n. 1. To roll out thin crisp cakes. 2. To undergo great hardships or sufferings; to labor hard on insufficient wages; to be unable to make both ends meet. 1. Ham ne to sadāṅ yehī pāpaṛ pīte haiṅ . Wom. 2. Ishq aisā nahīṅ kuchh khel ki har ek khele; Ham ne aṭhsaṭ baras is umr meṅ pāpaṛ bele . Raṅgīṅ. Love's not an easy game which every one can play, Years eight and sixty I have toiled in this essay.
subs: v.n. To roll out thin crisp cakes; to undergo great hardship or suffering; to labour hard on insufficient means; to eke out a scanty livelihood.

def: s.m. Crust, thin layer; scale, scab; thin crisp cake made of any pulse (esp. of mūṅg); thin light soil, fertile soil
more: s. m. A thin crisp cake made of any grain of the pea kind. پاپڙبيلنا pāpaṛ-belnā, To undergo great labour or pain.
subs: v.n. To roll out thin crisp cakes; to undergo great hardship or suffering; to labour hard on insufficient means; to eke out a scanty livelihood.

def: s.m. The plant Gardenia latifolia, &c. ( = pāpṛā, q.v.). s.m. The plant Gardenia latifolia; the plantain, Musa paradisiaca; — the fruit of the ḍhāk tree (also written pappaṛ, papaṛ, papuṛā)
subs: and pāpaṛ-khār (S. parpaṭa+kshāra), s.m. Ashes of the plantain tree used instead of salt for seasoning the cakes called pāpaṛ.

def: s.m. The name given in Southern India to the Pompelmose or Pumelo (in Northern India it is called ćakotrā).
adj. & s.m. Sinful, wicked, bad, guilty (of, -kā); cruel, hard-hearted; — sinner, criminal.
more: adj. Sinful; cruel; hard-hearted. Pāpī papīhā piyā piyā bole, Bhari bhari āweṅ hamrī chhatyāṅ . Wom. Song. Ṭhumrī. 'My love,' 'my love,' the cruel cuckoo oft doth cry, my heart is full to bursting nigh. pāp'ī , n. m. pāpan , n. f. A sinner. 1. Lakṛī jal koëlā bhaī, koëlā jal bhayā rākh, Maiṅ pāpan aisī jalī koëlā bhaī, na rākh . Dohā. The wood did burn and coal become, the coal to ashes turned, But I, a sinner, burn and burn, to coal nor ashes turned. 2. Pāpī kī nāo bhar-ke ḍūbe . Prov. The sinner's vessel fills and sinks. mahā-pāpī , n. m. A great sinner. Mere saṅg kī do do khilāweṅ, merī rīti god, phaṭe chhātī, Gaunā kar de bāp, mahā pāpī . Song. Children some two to four my co-mates' breasts have nursed, My lap alone all empty lies, my full breasts burst, Bring me my spouse, O cruel father sin-accurst!
adj. m. A sinner, a criminal.

def: s.m. = papīhā, q.v.

n. m. S. पपैया The black and white crested cuckoo. It is common all over India and breeds in the nests of the grey babblers. Are, papaiyā bāore! to he samjhāve kaun! Pī mero, maiṅ pīū kī, tu pī pī kare so kaun? Dohā. Out on thee, cuckoo! art thou mad? who could have taught thee so? My pī (beloved) is mine, and I am his, whom pī call'st thou?
s. m. A sparrow-hawk. (Falco nisus).
s.m. A species of cuckoo, Cuculus melanoleneos (syn. ćāṭak); — a child's whistle ( = papaiyā).
adj. & s.m. = pāpī, q.v. adj. & s.m. Sinful, wicked, bad, guilty (of, -kā); cruel, hard-hearted; — sinner, criminal.
more: adj. f. A sinner, criminal, wicked (woman).

def: s.f. contrac. of next, q.v.
s.f. Female delinquent, wicked woman.
more: adj. f. A sinner, criminal, wicked (woman).

def: adj. (superl.), Most wicked; very wicked or sinful.
adj. & s.m. = pāpī, q.v. adj. & s.m. Sinful, wicked, bad, guilty (of, -kā); cruel, hard-hearted; — sinner, criminal.
s.m. A species of Ricinus (?); the papan ( = papaiyā, q.v.). s.m. The Papaw tree, and its fruit, Carica papaya; a child's whistle ( = papīhā).
adv. On foot; afoot; walking.
s.f. Eyelash (syn. palak).
more: n. f. S. पपोटा Pr. pamho . The eyelash ( palak ).

def: v.n. To munch or mumble, to masticate (as an old man does who has no teeth; cf. polā).
more: v. n. To clump (an old man without teeth). Buḍḍhoṅ kī tarah papoltā hai .

def: n. f. A shoe ( jūtā ); slipper. pā-posh pe mārnā , v. n. To care for nothing; make light of. pā-posh se, pā-posh kī nok se . See balā se .
s.m. Eyelid.
n. m. The eyelid.
s.f. A kind of tree, Paretta indica(?), and its fruit (the leaves of the tree are used for poultices).
n. f. A plant, the leaves of which are used as poultice.
s.m. The plant Gardenia latifolia; the plantain, Musa paradisiaca; — the fruit of the ḍhāk tree (also written pappaṛ, papaṛ, papuṛā)
more: s. m. A certain plant. (Gardenia latifolia. Roxb. ).
subs: and pāpaṛ-khār (S. parpaṭa+kshāra), s.m. Ashes of the plantain tree used instead of salt for seasoning the cakes called pāpaṛ.

def: s.m. Crust, &c. = pāpaṛ, q.v. s.m. Crust, thin layer; scale, scab; thin crisp cake made of any pulse (esp. of mūṅg); thin light soil, fertile soil
subs: v.n. To roll out thin crisp cakes; to undergo great hardship or suffering; to labour hard on insufficient means; to eke out a scanty livelihood.

def: n. m. 1. A plant; Gardenia latifolia . 2. The fruit of the Ḍhāk tree. pāpṛā-khār, pāpaṛ-khār , n. m. The ashes of the plantain tree ( Musa paradisiaca ), used instead of salt for seasoning cakes called pāpaṛ ; pellitory root. (?).
s. m. Ashes of the plaintain tree (Musa paradisiaca) used instead of salt for seasoning the cakes called پاپڙ pāpaṛ.
v.n. = papṛiyānā, q.v. v.n. To form a crust or scab, &c.; to scale; to crystallize ( = papṛī jamnā).
v. n. 1. To form a crust. 2. To crystalize. Sāre hoṅṭoṅ pe papṛī jam gaī .
s.f. Thin crust, incrustation; scab, scale, scurf; scales formed by the drying up of the moist earth; incrustation formed on any liquid; thin, crisp cake (made of flour, &c.)
more: s. f. 1. A scab, a scale, scurf. 2. Scales formed by the drying up of moist earth. 3. Thin cakes of bread.
subs: v.n. To form a crust; to crystallize: papṛiy-ā-kat or kath, s.f. A kind of white-coloured kath or extract of Mimosa catechu.

def: n. f. S. पपड़ाना 1. A thin crust; incrustation; scab; scale. 2. Scales formed by the drying up of moist earth. 3. An incrustation formed on any liquid.
adj. (f. -ī), Scabby, scaly, scurfy.
s. f. A kind of whitecoloured kath (extract of Mimosa catechu).
v.n. To form a crust or scab, &c.; to scale; to crystallize ( = papṛī jamnā).

n. m. Leprosy and syphilis, considered as the punishment of sin in a former birth. pāp kāṭnā, harnā, yā dūr karnā , v. a. 1. To wipe out (one's sins). 2. To grant absolution or deliverance from sin. pāp kaṭnā, yā dūr honā , v. n. To be wiped out (one's sin). Hindū ko dharam baṛo Gangā, re baṛo Gangā, Darshan hīṅ se pāp kaṭat haiṅ, nhāë se chaṅgā . Rus. song. The Hindū's faith in Ganges river's great, in Ganges great! With offerings sins wiped off, upon its waters health doth wait. pāp-girah, pāpak-girah . Any ill-omened aspect of the stars, as the conjuction of the sun or Budh with the moon in her last quarter. Rāhū; Saturn; Mars. pāp lagānā , v. a. To charge; impeach; calumniate. pāp-nāshī , adj. Sin-destroying; purifying. Kāshī, pāp nāshī .
s.m. The plant Gardenia latifolia, &c. ( = pāpṛā, q.v.). s.m. The plant Gardenia latifolia; the plantain, Musa paradisiaca; — the fruit of the ḍhāk tree (also written pappaṛ, papaṛ, papuṛā)
subs: and pāpaṛ-khār (S. parpaṭa+kshāra), s.m. Ashes of the plantain tree used instead of salt for seasoning the cakes called pāpaṛ.

def: s.m. & adv. (gov. gen. with -ke, if the postp. is expressed), Further bank, or opposite shore or side (of a river, lake, &c.); the other side, concluding bound; the end or limit of anything, termination, conclusion; utmost limit or extent or reach; bottom, innermost depth, inmost recess; first turning up or ploughing of land; — over, across, on or to the other or further side, through, beyond
more: n. m. S. पार limit. 1. The opposite bank or shore; the other side. Pahoṅchā hai koī pār, bhaṭakta hai koī wār, Baiṭhā hai koī aish meṅ, phirtā hai koī zār . The world. Nazīr. Some have the river crossed, some wandering are this side, Some steeped in pleasure are, and some in sorrow bide. 2. A village or town on the other side of a river. 3. The end; termination; conclusion; period; finish. 4. The bottom; innermost depths; inmost recesses. 5. The first poughing of land. pār , adv. Across; on, or to the other, or further side; through; beyond. pār utārnā yā lagānā , v. a. See pār utarnā , v. n. pār utarnā , v. n. 1. To cross; go across; reach the end. 1. Apnī karnī, pār utarnī . Prov. Be good your deeds, your vessel speeds (across the Styx). 2. Karyā bāman, gor chamār, tekrā saṅg na utre pār . Prov. A Brahman black, a cobbler fair, deal with them and you'll prosper ne'er. (Pure Brahmans ar…
n. f. A scaffolding; stage; frame-work; the wooden frame over a well ( kaṭkaṛ ).

s. m. The opposite bank or shore; adv. Over, across, on or to the other side, through, beyond; adj. Last, past. پارکرنا pār-karnā, 1. To ferry over, to cross. 2. To finish, accomplish, carry through. 3. To perforate, to transfix. 4. To relieve. 5. To carry off.
subs: v.t. To put or take across, to put or ferry over; to carry through, cause to reach the end, &c. (see next)
v.n. To go or get across (esp. the ocean of existence), to cross; to reach the end; to accomplish, get through; to succeed, gain one's end
v.n. To get to the bottom (of), to fathom, to sound

Powerful. par jalnā , v. n. lit . to be burned (feathers). To be unable to get access to. 1. Yahāṅ achchhe achchhoṅ ke par jalte haiṅ . The feathers of the best are singed here. 2. 1. (for पर ) adj. Distant, remote, far; other, strange, foreign. پرديس par-des, Abroad. adv. and conj. Over, above, through, after, at, by, for, of; but, yet, however. 2. (for उपरि ) prep. On, upon, at, notwithstanding. 3. pari ( परि ) prep. in compos. About, around, round about, entirely: (Greek περὶ ). 4. pra ( प्र ) prep. in compos. def: adj. Immense, infinite

When small-pox rages may no evil shadow fall on my child! 2. To take after, or be like another. Is par nānī kā parchhāvāṅ paṛā hai . Wom. This child takes after his grandmother.

def: s.m. A curtain, screen, cover, veil, anything which acts as a screen, a wall, hangings, tapestry; film, fine web, pellicle, lid (of the eye); drum (of the ear); sail (of a ship); the piece which covers the chest in an angarkhā or coat; the surface of the earth; secrecy, privacy, modesty; seclusion, concealment; secret, mystery, reticence, reserve; screen, shelter, pretext, pretence; a musical tone or mode; a note of the gamut; the frets of a guitar, &c.
more: n. m. 1. A curtain. Jab parde sī * ghar ko āëṅ, Bāṅdhe aur laṭkāë jāëṅ . Riddle. Ans. Pardāh . The pardah sewn and brought home straight, nailed to the wall and hung his fate. 2. A screen; cover; veil; hangings; tapestry. 3. A small wall which screens the entrance to a house. 4. The piece which covers the chest in a native coat or aṅgarkhā . 5. The face of the earth; world. Dunyā ke pardah par mujh sī kam-bhakht na hogī . Mah. Wom. 6. Secrecy; privacy; concealment; seclusion. Agan koṭ meṅ ghar kīyo, jal meṅ kīyo nikās, Parde parde jāt hai pī apne ke pās . Riddle. Ans. Huqqe kā dhuāṅ . Smoke from a huqqā . She dwells in fire but through the water is her course, Close-veiled she hasteth to her lover's lips perforce. 7. Secret; mystery; reticence; reserve. 8. Screen; shelter; pretext; pretence; appearance. 9. A film; fine web; pellicle; lid (of the eye). 10. The drum (of the ear). 11. A sai…

s. m. 1. A curtain, a screen, cover, veil; secrecy, privacy, modesty. 2. A musical tone or mode. پرده پرده ديکهنا parda parda dekhnā, To pry or inquire secretly into any matter. پرده پرده کرنا parda parda karnā, To do a thing secretly or privately. پرده پوش parda-posh, Keeping a secret. پرده پوشي parda-posh-ī, The act of concealing a blemish. پرده چهوڙنا pardā chhoṛnā, 1. To let fall a curtain. 2. To lay aside concealment. پرده دار parda-dār, Confidential. پرده در parda-dar, A betrayer of secrets. پرده دري parda-dar-ī, The betraying of secrets. پرده رکهنا parda rakhnā, To give obscure hints, or to relate a matter in such terms as to be understood by part only of the hearers. پرده فاش parda-fāsh, Betraying secrets. پرده کرنا parda karnā, To conceal. پرده کسي کاپهاڙنا parda kisī kā phāṛnā, or پرده کسي کاکهولنا parda kisī kā kholnā, To reveal the faults of another. پرده نشين parda-nishīn, R…

def: n. f. Keeping one's secret; preserving confidence; faithfulness; fidelity. Bolī woh Bakāolī ki wārī! Mahram ‡ kā hai kām pardah-dārī , Wom. Nasīm. pardah-poshī karnā, pardah ḍhāṅknā , v. a. To hide or cover one's misdeeds. pardah chhoṛnā, yā ḍālnā , v. n. To draw the curtain. pardah ḍhaknā , v. n. To be covered (one's misdeeds in death). Ab to merā kahīṅ pardah ḍhak jāë! Wom. pardah fāsh karnā, pardah-darī k . v. a. 1. To disclose or betray one's secrets or misdeeds. 2. To lose one's credit; to be bankrupt. Kahe banenī, 'sun re banye, kyoṅ terā chitr udās?' 'Nigoḍḍī, rūī ne kar dīye parde phās! ' Play. The banyā's wife says 'why, my spouse, are you cast down?' Says he, 'you wretch, this cotton hath upon me blown.' ☞ He had on his hands a large stock of cotton purchased at a high rate during the American war. The point in the couplet turns on the conception that cotton, which is the stuffing of pardahs or screens, has been the means of exposing or betraying him. pardah karnā , v. n. To hide; to screen. Parde-wāloṅ, pardā kījo! Masons' cry. Pardah (veiled) women, look out! (A warning given by pasīs , masons, etc. about to ascend heights which overlook the yards of houses). pardah rakhnā , v. n. To keep one's secret; hide one's fault. parde lagnā , v. n. To sit behind the curtain; not to appear before strange men. (Ironically said of a woman who formerly went about openly). parde meṅ zardā lagānā,— —sūrākh k . v. a. To indulge secretly in licentious intercourse with strange men. (Said of a pardah-nashīn ). pardah-nashīn, parda-dār, parde-wālī; (Slang.) paṛ-dail , n. f. One who always remains behind the curtain and does not appear before strange men, and who is therefore excused from appearing in person in a court of justice. Dūṅ dil meṅ jagah kyoṅ nā maiṅ us parde-nashīṅ ko, Is se nahīṅ behtar koī parde kā makāṅ aur . Zafar. Should I not give my mistress veiled a place within my heart, No veil so closely drawn as she would wear within this heart.
